title: Handling 500 HTTP status code in HTTP Request node
symptom: When the HTTP Request node receives a 500 status code, the JSON data payload is not accessible as expected.
not_this_if: Error is not a 500 status code
Response options are not set to 'never error'
root_cause: The HTTP Request node in n8n treats responses with a 500 status code as errors by default, which prevents access to the response data payload. By changing the response options to 'never error', the node will allow access to the payload even when an error status is returned.
fix_steps: Open the HTTP Request node settings
Set the response option to 'never error'
Save the workflow and test again
agent_instruction: Change the response option in the HTTP Request node to 'never error' to access the payload.
